And so he's got a website set up and he came in and told me he was going to start focusing on SEO and all these things. And I kind of stopped and said, you know, those things are good and you should be doing them and focusing on them. But I said, if you want to make success and have money like today or tomorrow or start seeing results fast, you've got to learn to leverage email. It's the only thing you can get like the instant results. For example, we're launching, we launched about six months ago, we launched a new supplement and we've been testing the student really well. Now we're going through the rollout process. But you know, with that, like, I didn't know if it was going to be good or not at first and I could have went and done SEO and done all these things and eventually got people to my site, but I want to know today if it was going to make money. So boom, we went and we put up a quick site, we bought some emails, we drove some traffic and instantly we got some results. We found out what our conversions were and everything just worked fast because we used email. So email, it's really like the thing that we can all use to very, very quickly go and speed up our whole process. So there's a couple of different ways that we leverage email and that's what I'm going to be talking about today. All right, now, by far the, and there's basically three different paths that we're going to go down and there's different strategy behind each path. So the very first one, and this is, if you have this, I know some of you guys on this call do and some of you guys may not. And so, let's see. And so I want to kind of just put it out here first because this is the best you can have. In fact, let me kind of step back. This is fun. I'm building my new invisible funnel that I've been talking about over this process and I've been really taking a lot of time to take all the teachings and everything I've ever taught or thought or learned about internet marketing and putting it into a very simple system. One thing that I've been talking a lot about in this, the webinar that I'm getting closer to and finally being able to record is basically there are three types of traffic. Three types of traffic. I wish there's an easier way to draw on these things. Anyway, three types of traffic. So the first type of traffic is traffic that you control. So you hear that, what do you think about? For me, I think about traffic I can control. It means I don't own this traffic. I don't not own it by control. So if I go to Google and I pay for an ad, I'm controlling that traffic. I'm telling it where to go. If I go to buy a solo ad, I go and I tell that person where to send an email to. If I buy a ban or ad, that's traffic I control. I'm driving at some place and I control that traffic. Traffic control is really, really good. The second type of traffic is what I call traffic that you don't control. This is basically all those other things like SEO and social media and PR and things like that. I don't really control that traffic. If somebody goes to Google, type in a search and boom, they pop up on my site. I love that kind of traffic because you don't do anything for it. It shows up and that kind of stuff is fantastic. But I can't control that. So that's the second kind of traffic. And then the third type of traffic is traffic that you own. So that's the first part. The second thing I talk about in the training is that there's three types of websites. The first type of website is a squeeze page or a landing page or registration page. I'm sure you guys are most familiar with that. Very simple page. Usually it has a headline. You ask for somebody's email and then there's an easy submit button down there. The email and click submit and boom, that person joins your list. Now the power behind this is you take, here's this traffic you control. I've paid for an ad. I drive that traffic to a squeeze page. They give me their email address and what happens. That traffic instantly is converted over into traffic that I own. This is the best kind of traffic in the world. Now I don't have to pay for that person tomorrow. I can just send an email and boom, it goes out to that person. Traffic control. Next is traffic you don't control. The way that I target traffic I don't control, this is usually my website. It's like my blog. In fact, I'm rebuilding in 12 of our niches. We're in the process of rebuilding our blogs. They all do this. They have a video though and then there's an opt-in form where I'm asking them for their email address. Just like a squeeze page, but it's over here and then down below is like my blog where they can see all my stuff. Now this is traffic I don't control. So I'm blogging. I'm doing all that kind of stuff to build up my site, get more pages. Now I have some dude who comes and he goes to Google and types in whatever key or types in, you know, a kid that looks like he's 17 years old making money online and boom, my picture pops up. He clicks on it and he shows up in my site and the first thing in my goal is to get him to come right here and give me his email address and when he does, what happens? Boom, he turns into traffic that I own. Okay. And then the third type of website is pretty much any site I want. It could be a webinar. It could be a sales video. It could be whatever it is, but this is now, now that I own this traffic, now I can send emails to my list and I can send them wherever I want. Okay. So we start thinking about this. This is like for me, the process of how I build a business. Okay. First thing I do is I want to start buying ads and I start controlling traffic and my, my entire goal of buying traffic originally is not even making money. My goal of buying traffic is to turn that traffic into traffic I'm controlling and turning it into traffic that I own. As soon as you own that traffic, this whole game becomes easy. Okay. When I was driving traffic, I was controlling traffic and started building my squeeze page and started building my list. And first my list grew to 100 people and then to 500 and a thousand and 10,000 and 100,000 and 200,000 and 300,000. And the bigger that email list got, the more money I started making because basically, I got a point where I could send an email out to my list anytime I wanted and boom, money would start coming in. Okay. And you guys know that the goal is to transform all that traffic and traffic you own. Okay. And so that's really important. So that's what's happening next week. Now, the key when you have your email list is the sequence is happening on Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday and Thursday. Okay. Because if you want to get maximum people to come back here and join on Thursday, again, these guys are paying the $0 and that's going up to the $97. If you want maximum number of people for this, you got to do a cool sequence to get people to come back here. Okay. And so basically what we're doing is through Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, we're telling this story. Okay. This whole entire storytelling process that I talked to you about. So email number one that's going to go out to my own list is going to have a couple of elements, you know, usually starting at a point of high impact, back story and maybe the epiphany and I'll stop. Then we start talking about, hey, on this webinar over here, we're going to be talking more about it. We're going to be talking about the same thing. We start going through it and boom, we'll first people all the way until Thursday to get on this webinar. Okay. So we're telling our story about the webinar throughout this sequence until Thursday. Okay. That's kind of how that works. The other way you can do it, this is the way Diggs is doing his lately. I haven't done it this way. He may want to do this next week on ours too, so I'm not quite sure how it'll go. But what he's doing is, and it's really kind of interesting conceptually. And you can only do it with your own, obviously if you have your own email list. But he's coming in and on Monday, he's got a teaser about his story. So he doesn't tell him the whole story. He tells him about the story. And then Tuesday tells him about the story. And each of these days, he's pushing to a webinar. It's a free webinar. And that whole webinar is happening with, say, on Thursday. And so this is the one, and when they get on this webinar on Thursday, all this is, this is a completely free webinar. What he's doing on this webinar is he's telling the story. So he's telling the story that we told up here through email. He's telling it in a 90-minute webinar. And then at the end of that webinar, then he's coming back and pitching the $0 to $97 webinar. And that's the process that he's doing in his. And then from there, selling the back-end thing. So it'll be interesting. I'm not sure next week if we're going to do this model or this model, but it'll be fun either way. And speaking of, this is kind of interesting. For those who already have their own list or membership site, I shared this concept with somebody last two weeks or three weeks ago. I was in Oregon, or not Oregon, in, in Vail, Colorado. And I shared this invisible funnel concept with them. And that night they had a webinar with their members. I think they had four or 500 people who were on this webinar. And it was really interesting. So they were talking about, they were talking about what was happening. And then at the end of it, they're like, Hey, we're going to do this really cool webinar tomorrow if you're interested. And they just kind of pulled this out. They're like, this is how it works. It's $0. If you love it at the end, we'll be $9 to $7. We hate it. You don't do it, whatever. And of the 500 people they had online, they had over 50%. It's like 54% something like that that signed up, almost like 250, 300 people signed up just from a, just from this one little webinar mentioned at the very end. 50% of those people signed up for it. And I haven't followed them stats to see how they did on their back end sale, but they just put it together like in an hour. They just threw up a PayPal button. They talked about the members pushing the PayPal button and that was about it. But they were shocked at 50% of people took it because of, again, all this, this branding and positioning, all this stuff we're talking about back here, you guys with this, this whole like try before you buy model is amazing. You'll be shocked at how easy it is to get people to say yes and to want to be part of what it is you're doing. So anyway, so that's the first strategy is, is using your own email list and leveraging that. Okay. And so for some of you guys who have your own list, hopefully I gave you some ideas about how to do it, how to structure it the right way to get people to come in to sign up. So if you don't have an email list, you're looking to say, well, that's great Russell, but I don't have my own email list yet. What should I be doing? So that's the next thing to talk about is instead of leveraging your own email list, the next step in the process then is coming back and leveraging other people's email list. Okay. Through a process that we call joint ventures. Okay. Now with joint ventures, this is the topic that literally I had a friend and I, this is probably five years ago, we did an entire like six week course on how to get affiliates from all your products. We did a whole two or three day event. It was this big, huge long process. So I could talk about this for days and never get tired of it. But I just wanted to give you guys kind of the big, the big nuts and bolts behind it because one good joint venture can change your whole, your whole financial situation forever. We find the right people. So some of the things I want to mention just because like on my side, I get approached by joint ventures. I say on average, we get probably 30 requests a day if not more. We have standard requests. We send everybody, our standard responses and everybody. We don't even look at them anymore because we get so many of these requests coming through. Right. And so how do you, I want to talk about how do you stick out in the crowd? How do you get a joint venture answered when, you know, someone like me is getting 30 day, like how do you get some like me or whoever the person is in your, in your space that you want to connect with? How do you get through all that clutter and all that noise? And that's kind of, I think the most value I can give you because after that, that's just them sending email. So it was doing the same thing we talked about in the last step. But how do you, how do you get that? So the first thing is, there's a book that I bought a while ago. In fact, I'm in my library. I wonder if I could see it real quick. I've never actually read it, but I bought the book because the cover was so good that it kind of gave me the gist. And the cover, it said something, it was something like, dig your well before you're thirsty. I think I was the title of the book. And I read that and I said, first off, this guy deserves my money because he got me thinking about this concept. But the whole book was about joint ventures and partnerships. And what I find is that people come to me and in building a project, building a project and they're, can you wrestle, pro this thing? Because if you do, it'll make a bunch of money. Okay. That's the total wrong way to go about it. Your goal in this, if you want to do good joint ventures, your goal should not be like, let me find someone and figure out how to get them to promote my stuff. Because that's your mindset. It's really, really difficult because most people will say no to you because I still get in my situation with some people asking for that. It's tough. They're looking at people when they come up to you and you know that they're going to, they're in it because they want something for that. They're trying to get something out of you. And it's really, it's kind of frustrating. And so I look at the people I do joint ventures with and it's not the people who email me because they got a great offer. It's people who dig their well before they're thirsty. They build a relationship with me ahead of time. Okay. For example, I had a guy today who called me and said, I got this really cool webinar and he explained the whole thing. It was great and we're probably going to do a big promotion with him. But the reason why I even took his calls because for years he's been just a friend. He's been helping me. He thought, I thought how he could help me out. You know, he invited me and my family on a trip with them. Like just, he dug his well before he was thirsty. And now we've got such a good relationship that when he calls, I answer the phone. You want the favor. I just do it because that's kind of the way that it should work in this world. Right. And so it's very important to start, to start digging your well before you're thirsty. Now, how do you do that? There's a really, really good book. In fact, one of my favorite books in my library now is a book called The Ultimate Sales Machine by Chet Holmes. And Chet was a personal friend. He actually just passed away last summer. But just amazing guy. If you haven't read his book, The Ultimate Sales Machine yet, it's probably the best 20 bucks you'll ever spend on Amazon. Just an amazing, awesome book. And one of the core concepts I got out of there that he talked about is what he calls his dream 100. And so what he does is he goes out there and he says, okay, what's the market I want to be in? Who are my dream 100 partners? And he lists them out. Boom, this guy. And this guy. And this guy. He starts building out his list of his dream 100. Okay. And then he goes to those dream 100 and he starts trying this process, trying to build a relationship, trying to market to him, trying to become a relentless and get himself out there. So initially when he was starting his whole sales and consulting business, he was going after the Fortune 500 companies. He picked the 100, or maybe the Fortune 100, anyway, the top companies in the world, right? He went out there and he picked his dream 100. He's a 100 customer clients I want to work with. He started marketing to him. He'd send him a gift in the mail. Then he'd call him on the phone two weeks later. Then he'd send him another gift in the mail and then call him again. And eventually the gatekeepers keep seeing these gifts coming, right? They keep seeing phone calls and eventually within a two or three week period of time, boom, he gets through some doors and he's able to get appointments. And I think I can't remember the exact stats, but it was something like 63 of the top 100 companies that he went after. He was able to close as clients for his sales consulting. It was really inspiring when you think about that. And so that was the first step. And then a little while later, he talked about this in the book. He wrote a screenplay for a movie and he tried to pitch it to Hollywood and obviously he got no response. He said, look, I'm going to do the exact same thing. I'm going to build my dream 100 list of producers and of people that could take this script and turn it into something. He built that dream 100 list and then he started to do the same thing. He'd send all 100 people on the list a gift. And then next two weeks later, he'd call him on the phone. And eventually he got somebody in Hollywood to pick up a script. I think he retained the screen rights. He got a whole bunch of stuff out of it. A bunch of money up front. They built the whole movie for him and he got a whole bunch of credit on the back end. And he did that same process. And so I read that. I started thinking, man, I'm not really personally trying to close clients out there. That's not my goal, but I'm trying to close affiliates. My marketing needs to be targeted towards affiliates. And it was really interesting when I started thinking that way. In fact, I had a last summer, I had a chance to go to Kenya with my friends. One of my close friends started a charity called World Tea Trade. And so we had a chance to go to Kenya with him and build schools out there and just an amazing experience. And one of the guys that was out there, his name was Mark Jenny, and he runs a company called Brain Host. And I started talking to him about his company. He said, yeah, in the first year, we've got 300,000 hosting customers. I said, 300,000 customers? Like insane, 300,000 people paying him $20 a month for hosting. I knew the math in my head. I said, well, in a 12-month period of time, you built the company. It's doing $60,000,000,000. He kind of smiled. And the guy looks really young. I think he might be younger than me, even maybe my same age. I said, well, how did you market? How did you get 300,000 customers? He said, Russell, I don't focus on getting customers. He's like, I don't think I focus on getting affiliates. He's like, I built my list of all my dream affiliates, and I market aggressively to my affiliates. I send him gifts. I send him packages. I send him books. And he's like, that's all I do. And then my affiliates like me, and they print their list. And that's how I was able to build in 12 months, the company is doing $60 million online. And really, that is a pippy thing. Now, I need to start focusing more on marketing to my affiliates. Like those are the people that can drive sales. Those are the people I want doing this great stuff for us. And so that became my mind sense. We started focusing on it. We made our Dream 100 list. Who are top affiliates that we would like to promote our products? We picked those people. We started focusing on them. We started marketing to them. And that's kind of how we started growing a lot of these different companies that we have. And so each of my niche is one of the first things I do. When I get in there is I start building my Dream 100 list. We have a diabetic supplement. We're in the process right now. We have a baby sign language product. We're in the process right now. We have a Q-Party product. We have all these different products that we have. And in every one of those, one of the core things we're doing is building our Dream 100 list and then figuring out how to market those people, how to build relationships, get to know those guys, dig our well before we're thirsty. And that's kind of what we're doing. And it's working. It works great. Like if this was the only marketing strategy you guys did in your business, it could be huge. Now the big key with this is right now 100 people is a lot. And you probably don't want to do 100 people. You probably just want one or two. And so I started looking around like, look at who are people you already have relationships with? Or who do you have relationships with as a relationship with somebody else? Because all you really need is one good success story. We're an affiliate that promotes your product and it makes them and you a lot of money. If you get one good success story, it's so easy to go to the next affiliate. Okay? Because after you've got one really good, I mean, affiliates are, they want you to be their friends. But the second thing they want is they want money. So, I mean, that's part of the webinar he called me today. So right now this webinar is averaging $110 per attendee. I know, well, I can put on, I can probably put on $2,000, $3,000 attendees. That means I can make $200,000 to $300,000 per person's webinar. And that made me even more excited to take his call. Okay? So you start knowing your numbers, you can do that. Now initially you don't know your numbers. Okay? So you got to go out there and you got to find somebody, you got to find a partner who's willing to kind of take that and say, look, I don't know the numbers on this, it's brand new, but I want you as a partner. And if you'll promote it, this is all due in exchange. Okay? There's a lot of things. And every affiliate you find is going to be motivated by different things. Okay? If you had a affiliate who's your friend already, they may do it just because you're friends with them. Say, I got to see if I'm trying it out, would you be willing to promote it for me? And a lot of times if they're your friends, they're going to say yes. Okay? And sometimes they may not be your friends and they may not say yes. Okay? For example, we're launching our supplement, our diabetic supplement. So we can do our numbers, we kind of know what's happening. So I went to this guy who's one of the biggest media buyers in the diabetic industry. And the guy is insane. In the last six months, they spent over a million dollars on diabetic traffic alone. And so he could drive a lot of traffic for me. And I said, hey, I've got this new offer over here. It's awesome. I said, how much would you, how much, you know, I want you to test this and see if it'll work for me. And he's like, well, Russell, this is how it works. He's like, if you can guarantee me that I will make $2.25 for every quick that I send you, I'll promote as much as you want. I said, well, you know, I don't know if my, if my, if my offer is good enough to make $2.25. I have no idea if it will, it might make 50 cents a click. He said, I don't care. He's like, but if you can guarantee me $2.25, he's like, all of a sudden you bunch of traffic and we can start testing and find out. And so we came back and we spent $10,000 with him and he's in the process right now sending this traffic at $2.25 a click. Okay. Now a couple of things could happen. First off, my offer may bomb. Okay. And I guarantee the money upfront. I lost $10,000 upfront because I'm guaranteeing this, but if it works, I might get, you know, maybe I make $10 per click. Okay. It's something that becomes really, really cheap traffic, but I don't know that right now. So I have to go and basically guarantee his results ahead of time. Okay. So how does this translate to you guys in a visible funnel? Let's say you go to affiliate and say, look, I'm doing this really cool thing. And I love you sending email out to your list. And this is my first time I've done it. I've got no stats, but this is how it works. Basically they're going to come in here and they're going to sign up for a webinar. It's $0. And then afterwards we're going to bill them $97 if they love it. And my first time doing it, I think it's going to go really good. But you know what? I really want you to promote this. I just want to get some stats and some numbers. So if you be willing to promote this, I will give you 100% of everything this thing makes. Okay. So if you get in, if you get in 100 people to sign up for this thing and it's $100, what's that? $10,000. They make $10,000 bucks off. I think you keep 100% of it just because I appreciate you taking a risk on me. Okay. Now when you guarantee some results like that, they're going to be a lot more likely to say, yes, this guy, this diabetic guy will send you unlimited traffic for $2.25 a click. And that may be good for you, maybe bad for me, but I'm testing it right now and I'm going to find out my metrics with this traffic from there. Okay. Same thing for you guys. This person is taking a risk on you right now. So take that risk away from you. I'll take the risk, including on me, I'll give you 100% of what we make. I just want to see if this works. Okay. Or maybe come back and say, look, you send me a thousand clicks, I'll pay you a dollar clicker, I'll pay you 50 cents a clicker, I'll rate check for $500 if you do this for me. And you take the risk away from them, you'll be shocked at how many people will say yes to that. Okay. So this is an easy way if you don't have a list to start finding, start doing that. Okay. And people always ask me, well, how do I find JV partners? How do I find people that can promote my stuff? The big reality is, if you're new to marketplace, you need to get immersed in it. You need to start finding out who the players are. Okay. Every industry that I'm in at least has players, right? As the people that have the big list. And where do you find those people? Like go on Amazon, see who's writing books about your topic. Go to LinkedIn and see who's got big followings on LinkedIn. Go to Facebook, see who's got fan pages. Go to Google and see who's selling products. There's no one way to find it. It's just going out there and doing a little research, a little bit of work and see who else is selling to the same target customer that you have. And then start building relationships with them. I met a guy in January and he's built a multi-million dollar business. It's really interesting. All he does, he goes into LinkedIn and I've never even used LinkedIn. I don't know how it works, but I guess in LinkedIn you can see people have groups or followings or something, right? So if my people have a list of like 100,000 followers in LinkedIn and then he'll go to LinkedIn and say, look, you have a huge list, you're not monetizing right now. Let me help you monetize it. I've got this product over here. And usually he doesn't do it with his own products. He has other people's products. He says, promote this product to this person's list and will be a 50% commission. And he goes back to the product owner and he says, I can get you, I can give people from all this, I just want 10% in between. And he does his brokering deal and he makes 10%. And he's killing it. He's made a million dollars in the last year doing this. But you can do the same thing with your own product. And you'll find some of the big following in your same industry, your same LinkedIn and say, hey, you know, again, this is kind of the digging your well before you're thirsty thing, but go and get to know them, sign up for their group, provide some content, give them a call, find out who they are, what you can do to help them, what they're looking for in their life. If you can do a little background stuff like that, it becomes very, very simple and easy to start building that relationship. Okay. You'll be shocked like changing the conversation from, hey, I've got this really cool thing and you promote it to, hey, I love your stuff. You're amazing. Let me give you a testimonial and what can I do to help you out? I love your mission. I want to help you out. Like it changes the whole conversation and becomes so much easier to get those people to want to help you. Okay. So joint ventures are huge, you guys, and they're easy. They don't cost any money really if you do it correctly. But initially it's going to be this. It's going to be going out there and giving someone all the upfront money. Okay. Even say, look, the backup selling this thing, it's a $10,000 a weekend. I'll give you 90% out of 50% of it. Whatever it is you want. Okay. Because your goal on this is not to make money upfront. Your goal is just to see if it works. Because if it works, then it becomes really exciting because then this person agrees. You do that first promotion. Everything comes out good. Then you come back and say, look, so and so sent a thousand clicks. From a thousand clicks, he got 100 people who registered from the webinar. 100 people we had 90 who rebuild. That means we made, let's say, you know, say it was $100. That means we made $9,000 from it. And then on the back, we sold this. We sold two people that let's say it was $5,000. Two people made $10,000 on the back. So our total revenue was $19,000 from him sending a thousand clicks. Okay. Now, if this guy maybe you gave him 100% of it, but for JB partners, they look and he got 50%. I got 50%. That means from a thousand clicks, he made what's 19 divided by half. I had to pick a number that's not easy. 9,950, right? That you made 9,500 bucks. Affiliate commission for sending a thousand clicks. That means he averaged $9.50 for every click he sent. Okay. Now you showed affiliate that number right there. They will be lining up around the block to promote your stuff. Okay. If an affiliate can get over a dollar a click usually, they're excited. They get to $2 or $3 a click. They get really excited. You get something to $9 a click. They are going to go crazy and they will find you and they will beg to promote you every day. That's kind of the reality behind it. Okay. But you don't know these numbers until you get an affiliate to actually promote it for you. So go out there and let an affiliate, you know, again, that's worth you giving them 100% of all the money you make because it's their traffic anyway. You're learning this process, testing it out and boom, hoping it works. And if it does, boom, here's a big huge payday for you. Okay. And then you go out there and you ask this guy, what are the people that you know that have lists just like yours? And I'll say, here's five of my buddies and say, look, if you tell me five of your buddies, I'll give you 10% of any money that they make. And suddenly, boom, this guy introduced you to 10 other, 10 new people to do the webinar with. You give 50% to that person, 10% to this person for hooking you up. You keep 40%. And now you've got a business. Okay. You've got a business you can scale and you can rapidly grow. Does that make sense, you guys? Okay. And this is how I look at all my businesses. This is how it literally works. We find someone to test it. We give them all the profits. When it works, good. We get the numbers back and then we ask them, introduce us to their friends for 10% and it grows from there. We did a webinar the other day with a girl named Brittany Lynch and her webinar for whatever reason did amazing. Our commission check was $63,000 the first time. And I was ecstatic. We've done that three times since then. Every time she was like 50,000 to 60,000 bucks. But after I was done, that's what she came to me. She said, look, do you have anyone else that can run this webinar? I just did it against your list. I'll give you 10%. Immediately, I picked up my phone. I called like 10 of my buddies like, Brittany's webinar is killing it. You need to do it. Brittany's webinar is killing it. Call every single one of them. And they all did, they all ran the webinar. Every single one of you will got the same commission, 50, 60 grand. Was there a commission check? I got 10% of that. So I got five grand, five grand, four grand, six grand, boom, boom, boom. And I made another, I can't remember the number, probably $30,000 or $40,000 on my side by making 10 phone calls. And it was awesome. And they loved me and everybody is, Brittany loved me. The JV partner loved me. Everybody worked because all I did was just tell people about something that worked for me. Okay. That's the mindset he's affiliated with you guys. And so again, all you gotta do is find one person to say yes. And then it opens up. And as long as obviously this does well, you know, that's the one caveat. If I one person to say yes, you do this process and if it works well, then you can really scale it out and grow it quickly. Okay. All right. So that is the affiliate stuff I wanted to kind of talk about. Did I get you guys excited? Did you see kind of the process in my mind that I go through when we're launching companies and launching new offers? Because you can see that process, you can copy it. I'm very big in like just finding out people's processes and modeling it. And, you know, this is my process, you guys. You can get my office, you see this. I mean, we were brainstorming Dream 100 list today for our diabetic supplement. Like that is the exact same thing we're doing is what we're talking about with you guys. So anyway, okay. So that's the second one. Now the third one, and this is the one that somebody is going to revert to you because the JV one takes a little while. You got to build relationships and time and stuff like that. But again, it's nice because it becomes free traffic primarily. Then the third one to talk about is, back here, number three is basically coming back and doing solo ads. Now what a solo ad is, is basically similar to a JV where somebody's got a big email list and they go and they, you write an email, you give it to them with your product and they send the email out to the list and any money you make, you get a key. You keep 100% of it, but you pay them for that ad. Okay. Just like if you were going to go buy a direct mail letter, right? You go, you find a list that you like, you write your postcard, your direct mail piece, you send it to the list broker, you pay the guy his money for his list, they send the thing out and you keep all the money that comes in. Okay. So solo ads are great. In fact, we've primarily for the last, in our internet marketing business, we've pretty much lived off solo ads for the last 12 months or so. We've been a lot more solo ads than we have joint ventures. It's been fantastic. It's been great for us. So that's kind of the concept here, solo ads. Now, the way solo ads kind of came about, at least in the internet marketing industry, but I'm going to show you this stuff works in pretty much every industry, is there's a whole bunch of people who have big email lists. For a long time, they did joint ventures, but sometimes the joint ventures, you kind of get the short end of the stick. Sometimes somebody tells you it's converting great and then they promote and nothing happens. Or they promote bunch of people by it and there's a lot of refunds. It's a lot of people that were doing joint ventures got really frustrated and said, look, I'm only going to do this if there's a way that I can guarantee I'm going to get paid upfront. So they came back and said, look, instead of you just splitting money 50-50 with me, pay me $1,000 and I'll send the email to my list. And that's kind of where joint ventures, or the solo ads kind of started. And it's now become a huge industry because a lot of people just like, they get guaranteed money. They may make less money, but they get it guaranteed. And like there's a whole world of people that live and die by doing solo ads. And so it's very simple. Now I'm going to show you guys a couple of websites. If you're in the internet marketing, maybe you work at home, niche or anything kind of bleeds over into that. This is, if you're an affiliate incubator.com, this is my affiliate program site we run our affiliate program off of. You come out here and you go to traffic sources and scroll out of solo ads. Here's a resource of dozens and dozens of close to 100 people who do solo ads. Okay. Now this first thing at the top called click agent solos. This is only great if your offer is super, super, super biz off. Okay. Which is like, I don't know, like, so I don't think this is going to work for most of you guys, but don't use click agent unless again, you're super, super biz off. Next thing here, this is solo ad list brokers. Now these are similar. If you do direct mail like a list broker, these guys basically go through a lot of different email lists and then you can rent them from them. These lists are traditionally bigger and they traditionally cost a little more money. First one is Brian Littman. And just to be fair to you guys, we used to use Brian a lot in the past. We don't use him nearly as much now. I don't think the quality of the list he manages are as good as they used to be. But what's nice about him is he does manage a lot of lists like in the personal development space. And so if you click here, it takes you to their site. You can contact Brian and get to know him and basically you show him your offer and he'll say, this is the list link with you. Now one thing he's going to try to do, his brother, Mike, Mike Littman has a list and that's his favorite one to sell because he gets the highest commission. So he's always going to try to sell you the Mike Littman list. Usually the Mike Littman list works really good. The first email you send out and not so good the second email you send out for whatever reason. So that's what's going to hit you on. Just be aware of that. If you click here, it shows you they have a lot of personal development lists and business opportunity lists, finance lists, health and relationship lists. And so they have some good ones. So this would be my first choice, but if you want to, if you're trying to scale something, I'd go to dedicated emails to Brian Littman and just talk to them and you can get a lot of lists from them. Okay. Now this is one, this is Dagan's ad and private agency. And this is only though for like work at home type offers. If you go to his, in fact, all you guys should go here anyway. It's the homebusinessagency.com. Basically squeeze page. You come down here, you put your name in your email address in. And then what happens is, and this is really cool is usually once a day, Dagan, well, let me step up. So Dagan spends about 100 to 150 grand a month in solo ads. He buys them at the very beginning of every month. And then because he buys in such bulk, it's huge discounts. So he tests, he tests a solo ad. And if it does good to his list, then he goes back to that solo ad provider and say, look, I want to buy nine of these for my members. They hugely discounted price. And he buys them. Then he goes to this list. He sends email out saying, okay, today we've got, there's four solo ads available. This is how many clicks you get. This is how it did to my list. And if you want to buy it, there's four spots. And when they're gone, they're gone. And usually within like five minutes, those things sell out. Like I've, we try to buy them every day. We miss a lot of them because they just sell out too fast. Okay. And so, but it's a good list to be on because every day you'll see like what the hot deals are. He always tests them first. The list quality is always awesome. And, and he always gets your discount. So I'd recommend the home business agency. The biggest goal though is not to go just blowing money up for us all about testing, test small and go bigger. People are always going to want you to like buy their biggest packages, but just realize you don't have to come in and say, look, I just want to test this out and see if it works. If it works, I will buy this thing every day for you for the rest of your life. You're going to make a ton of money, but let me just test it to make sure you're not scamming me. Okay. And test it out and it may or may not work, but that's kind of how you start testing things out really, really quickly. Okay. All right. So let me know if you guys have any questions so far. We talked about, we kind of covered the, the core and what we use for email. Again, my favorite email is it's not technical. It's not like the stuff I'll be talking about in the next training webinars is a little more technical. Like you got to learn how to do banner ads and you're, you're, there's all these pieces that go into it. Well, this is a lot simpler. You either have a list or you find someone's got a list to become partners with or you buy ads in somebody's list and they all kind of go together really easily. Okay. Um, one other trick, by the way, we do a lot of times as a solo ad is, um, after we buy a solo ad and we'll promote our product, then I take my numbers and I go back to that person said, gee, see this? Like I gave you a thousand bucks, but I ended up making like 8,000 because of your ads. So just think so much. And then at first I was like, wow, I'm, I, you know, that's not good. Then they come back to look, if you want to become an affiliate, you can give me to fill all the 50% commission. So you would have made four grand on that. You would have made by promoting that. I'll make you to fill your purchasing every day if you want. And we've got people in the past who, uh, had such success with ours. They go to our site, they, they like take down all the rest of their banners. They put our banner ads everywhere. They plaster all over the site. They start emailing our thing a couple of times a week and we never had to pay for ads again. Okay. Because, um, we were able to show them that, Hey, you made a lot more money just promoting my thing. Why don't you just promote my thing? And, uh, it works really, really good. So just that kind of a thought out there. Okay. Daryl's asking a question. He said, Hey, Ruff, we're being in Australia. There's a big time difference. When do you think it's the best time to live webinar if your customers are predominantly American based? Um, that's a good question. Um, and a lot of it really does depend on, on your market. Like the internet marketing market, um, we do webinars a lot of times during the afternoon and people show up because a lot of people are working from home and they can just do that. Right. Um, but other markets like, um, um, for the other ones that we've done it in, like the, the dating market is traditionally, it's got to be nighttime because that's when those dudes are home, like wishing they had a girlfriend. Um, but it doesn't work good during the day because they're all working. Right. So a lot of it is just more market specific. Um, and then, so what I would probably do is like the first one or two you're doing that are more than live, like, like, I would probably, um, do that more like during the, like lunch hours I really like, because you tell people like, Hey, just get off lunch early and come this thing or do you take your lunch break long or whatever. And we get a lot of people there. In fact, we found better attendance during lunch hour ones now that we do it our nighttime ones, which is kind of interesting to me, cause I say lunch hours, but then after we, we have it automated for you guys. Uh, when we have it automated, it was really cool as the basic, they take the times when they want to be in it and it's local to that person. So if they're in Jamaica and they want to look at five o'clock of their time, then boom, it'll play at five o'clock of their time. You know what I mean? And so you can do a lot more thinking when it's automated. Um, but for this one, I would probably look at like American lunch hours. Um, you should probably the best. Uh, if you're in business opportunities, um, if, if you're like, if it's like, hardcore bizop, like, you know, like when people are brand new beginners, like usually I think nights are better. It's more like entrepreneur based, um, where you look for some of the audience kind of working for themselves. Uh, I'd say lunchtime is probably the best for those kind of people. Okay.
